paper_title: New Examples of Minimal Non-Strongly-Perfect Graphs
summary: The conjecture suggests that characterizing outerplanar strongly perfect graphs (equivalently, listing all minimal non-strongly-perfect outerplanar graphs) is a tractable subproblem, even though a full characterization of minimal non-strongly-perfect graphs appears out of reach. No follow-up paper resolving or making substantial progress on this specific conjecture was found in indexed literature through May 2026.
search_queries: outerplanar strongly perfect graphs characterization minimal non-strongly-perfect
Chudnovsky Dibek Seymour strongly perfect outerplanar 2003.01846
notes: No follow-up paper addressing outerplanar strongly perfect graphs was found. The conjecture is informal (no precise statement is given in the truncated PDF source) and relatively recent (2020); absence of follow-up is consistent with it remaining open. The published journal version appeared in Discrete Mathematics (S0012365X21000479) but was behind a paywall and could not be fetched.
